project: Animal eukaryotic nucleus Blender-to-Roblox functional model

components.name: Cell_Context_RootRig
components.purpose: Defines the whole animal cell coordinate system so the nucleus is cohesive with the larger Roblox cell.
components.geometry: Create a transparent flattened ovoid cell shell and a central origin/pivot marker. Keep it lightweight.
components.animation: Cell-level parent; no biological animation required except optional subtle cytoplasm drift.
components.pass: Whole cell silhouette is visible and nucleus sits inside it, not as a floating unrelated model.

components.name: Cytoplasm_Volume
components.purpose: Shows that the nucleus is embedded in cytoplasm and that cargo moves between nucleus and cytoplasm.
components.geometry: Low-poly transparent ovoid volume with faint particle dots and flowing curves.
components.animation: Subtle slow drift of internal particles and cytoplasmic paths.
components.pass: Does not obscure the nucleus; should be readable from isometric and side camera views.

components.name: NucleusSocket_Center
components.purpose: Roblox assembly anchor for placing, repositioning, or replacing the nucleus model inside the cell.
components.geometry: Small non-render or optional visible ring at nucleus center; add XYZ arrow axes.
components.animation: Parent point for the whole nucleus. All nucleus objects should ultimately track this pivot.
components.pass: Origin is at the center of nucleus mass; rotations preserve envelope alignment.

components.name: Nucleus_RootRig
components.purpose: Parent object for all nucleus assets; controls placement, scaling, and high-level animation.
components.geometry: Visible pivot gizmo plus optional faint sphere radius guide.
components.animation: Master parent for interphase, transport, and mitosis actions.
components.pass: Every nucleus component is parented under this root; no stray unparented nucleus objects.

components.name: NE_OuterMembrane_Panels
components.purpose: Outer nuclear membrane; continuous with rough ER; splits during mitosis.
components.geometry: Create 8-12 curved shell panels forming outer sphere. Leave circular holes or socket depressions for NPCs. Add small surface bumps.
components.animation: Panels separate outward during nuclear envelope breakdown; reassemble during telophase.
components.pass: Double membrane is clear; outer layer is distinct from inner membrane; panel seams are visible but not distracting.

components.name: NE_InnerMembrane_Panels
components.purpose: Inner nuclear membrane lining nucleoplasm; supports lamina attachment.
components.geometry: Create matching inner shell panels with slightly smaller radius and darker purple color. Align with outer panels but keep gap visible.
components.animation: Splits with envelope but slightly delayed behind the outer membrane.
components.pass: Perinuclear space visibly separates inner and outer membranes throughout the shell.

components.name: NE_PerinuclearSpace_LumenBand
components.purpose: Narrow lumen between inner and outer membranes, continuous with ER lumen.
components.geometry: Thin amber/cyan translucent spherical band between the two membranes. Use additive material.
components.animation: Glows subtly; splits with envelope panels during mitosis.
components.pass: Learner can see there are two membranes rather than one thick wall.

components.name: NPC_RingAssemblies
components.purpose: Nuclear pore complexes that regulate transport; not simple holes.
components.geometry: Place 24-36 pores across envelope. Each pore is a torus-like ring with 8 radial protein lobes, cytoplasmic rim, nuclear rim, and central opening.
components.animation: NPC rings remain aligned to membrane; can detach/scatter in mitosis; pulse during transport.
components.pass: NPCs read as protein assemblies with ring/lobes, not blank circular holes.

components.name: NPC_GateCore_Selectivity
components.purpose: Shows selective barrier inside each NPC.
components.geometry: For major visible NPCs, add translucent gel-like plug or star/funnel fibers in the pore center.
components.animation: Gate opens/pulses for import/export cargo; small solutes can pass freely while large cargo uses marked transport.
components.pass: At least six visible NPCs show gate cores clearly from the main camera.

components.name: NuclearLamina_LatticeTiles
components.purpose: Supportive lamin network under inner membrane; maintains nuclear shape and anchors chromatin.
components.geometry: Gold/orange geodesic net just inside inner membrane. Use curves or low-poly tubes.
components.animation: Disassembles into short fragments before envelope breakdown; reassembles in telophase.
components.pass: Lamina is visually under the inner membrane, not floating in the nucleoplasm center.

components.name: Nucleoplasm_Volume
components.purpose: Semi-fluid internal matrix containing enzymes, nucleotides, proteins, RNA, chromatin, and nucleolus.
components.geometry: Transparent bluish sphere/ellipsoid inside inner membrane with tiny floating particles.
components.animation: Slow fluid-like movement and gentle particle drift.
components.pass: Readable as internal volume; does not hide chromatin or nucleolus.

components.name: Chromatin_DiffuseThreads
components.purpose: DNA packaged with histones during interphase; source of transcription.
components.geometry: Create layered irregular purple/cyan thread network distributed inside nucleoplasm, with denser regions near lamina and around nucleolus.
components.animation: Idle drifting; selected regions glow for transcription; condense toward chromosome meshes during mitosis.
components.pass: Chromatin remains inside nucleus. It should never be animated leaving through NPCs.

components.name: Chromosomes_Condensed_MitosisSet
components.purpose: Condensed DNA state for mitosis animation.
components.geometry: Create 6-10 stylized X/rod chromosome meshes for educational display, initially hidden inside nucleus.
components.animation: Fade/scale in during prophase as diffuse chromatin fades; align for metaphase demo if requested.
components.pass: Chromosomes have clear condensed forms and are separate from interphase chromatin meshes.

components.name: Nucleolus_SubregionBody
components.purpose: Dense non-membrane nuclear region for rRNA synthesis and ribosomal subunit assembly.
components.geometry: Create one large irregular purple spheroid with three subtle subregion textures: fibrillar center, dense fibrillar component, granular component.
components.animation: Pulses during rRNA production; fades/disassembles during mitosis; reforms after envelope reassembly.
components.pass: No membrane is drawn around it; label/material should communicate it is a dense region, not a separate organelle membrane.

components.name: TransportCargo_Markers
components.purpose: Shows protein import and RNA/ribosomal export pathways through NPCs.
components.geometry: Green protein cargo spheres with NLS tags move inward; pink RNA squiggle curves and magenta ribosomal subunit particles move outward.
components.animation: Create path curves through selected NPCs. Protein moves cytoplasm->nucleoplasm; RNA moves nucleoplasm->cytoplasm.
components.pass: DNA never exits; only RNA/protein/ribosomal cargo crosses NPCs.

components.name: OuterEnvelope_ERConnectionPort
components.purpose: Shows outer nuclear membrane continuity with rough ER.
components.geometry: Add 2-3 ribosome-dotted ER sheet/tube segments attached to one side of outer membrane.
components.animation: Optional subtle ER sway; remains attached to outer membrane in interphase; separates during envelope breakdown.
components.pass: Connection side is visible and clear without overwhelming nucleus.

components.name: NE_BreakdownFragments_Vesicles
components.purpose: Represents nuclear envelope breakdown into vesicles/fragments during mitosis.
components.geometry: Small purple membrane shards and vesicle spheres, initially hidden around envelope.
components.animation: Appear as outer/inner membrane panels fade/split; scatter outward, then hide/reverse for reassembly.
components.pass: Fragment motion is organized enough to teach breakdown, not random visual noise.

components.name: Nucleus_InteractionHotspots
components.purpose: Roblox interactions for learning, inspection, and triggering animations.
components.geometry: Small low-poly icon plaques/rings: Transport, Transcription, Mitosis, Envelope, Nucleolus, Chromatin.
components.animation: Pulse gently; each hotspot triggers one educational animation group.
components.pass: Hotspots are visible but do not block scientific structures.

Membrane_Outer_Purple.finish: stylized membrane, satin, procedural fine bump

RoughER_BluePurple.finish: folded membrane sheets with ribosome dots

animation_ranges.name: A01_Interphase_Idle
animation_ranges.description: Nucleoplasm particles drift, chromatin gently wiggles, nucleolus pulses, NPC gates breathe lightly.

animation_ranges.name: A02_Protein_Import_NLS
animation_ranges.description: Green NLS protein cargo moves from cytoplasm through NPC gate into nucleoplasm; gate pulses open during crossing.

animation_ranges.name: A03_RNA_Export
animation_ranges.description: Pink mRNA squiggle and ribosomal subunit particles move from nucleoplasm through NPC to cytoplasm; DNA/chromatin stays inside.

animation_ranges.name: A04_Transcription_Hotspot
animation_ranges.description: A chromatin region glows; small RNA curve appears and travels toward an NPC.

animation_ranges.name: A05_Mitosis_Envelope_Breakdown
animation_ranges.description: Chromatin condenses; nucleolus fades; lamina fragments; NPCs detach; envelope panels separate into membrane fragments.

animation_ranges.name: A06_Nuclear_Reassembly
animation_ranges.description: Fragments and panels return; lamina re-forms; NPCs reinsert; nucleolus returns; chromatin diffuses.
